title: Derris piscatoria
intro: Derris piscatoria, also known as Derris elliptica, is a leguminous plant native to tropical Asia and Australia, belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties. The bioactive constituents of Derris piscatoria include rotenone, a natural insecticide, and other alkaloids, such as derricin, ellipticine, and derritoside, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Derris piscatoria include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat fever, inflammation, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Derris piscatoria is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Leguminosae, commonly known as Fabaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Derris, which consists of a group of tropical plants native to the Indo-Pacific region. Derris piscatoria is a climber or vine, and its classification is further supported by its distinct characteristics and distribution patterns. The species is found in countries such as India, Sri Lanka, and Southeast Asia, where it grows in humid and subtropical environments. Derris piscatoria is also known for its use as a natural insecticide and in traditional medicine.
uses: Derris piscatoria, a plant native to tropical Africa, has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The extracts from the plant have been applied to wounds and skin irritations to accelerate healing and reduce the risk of infection. In addition to its external uses, Derris piscatoria has been used to treat fever and other systemic illnesses. The plant's roots and leaves contain compounds with potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Derris piscatoria.
benefits: Derris piscatoria is known to have various health benefits, particularly in traditional medicine. constituents: Derris piscatoria contains active constituents such as rotenone, a natural insecticide, and derrisine, a toxic compound. These constituents contribute to the plant's insecticidal properties. The rotenone found in Derris piscatoria is primarily responsible for its insecticidal effects, making it a popular choice for pest control. Derris piscatoria's active constituents are derived from its roots and have been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The plant's toxic compounds have also been used as a fishing poison in some cultures.

side_effects: Derris piscatoria is primarily known as a source of rotenone, a toxic compound used as an insecticide and piscicide. However, exposure to rotenone through Derris piscatoria can have severe side effects on humans and animals. Prolonged exposure may cause headaches, dizziness, and nausea, while high levels of rotenone have been linked to liver damage and cancer in animals. Ingestion of Derris piscatoria can also lead to gastrointestinal problems, including vomiting and diarrhea. It is essential to handle Derris piscatoria with caution and follow proper safety protocols to minimize exposure risks.
